hard.ais_why:
• On the 12/18/2025 call, champion Karen Pagorek pushed Arthur to define autonomous and agentic terms more precisely, specifically asking him to disentangle e-auctions, tactical spend, and autonomous sourcing with negotiation inside iSource. That terminology pressure is substantive AI dialogue from the buyer side, not surface-level interest.
• Karen then asked what a pilot might look like. Arthur proposed pilots covering iSource, autonomous sourcing, and spend analysis: a sandbox RFX event, an MRO category negotiation, and a 3-month spend cube prototype. Karen also pressure-tested ROI realism on the $48M savings claim, calling for transparency on the assumptions.
• The offsetting signal is that the three-prong AI POC was paused on 5/11/26. That is a real down-signal post-call. The buyer is still at "quantify incremental value" and remembers poor feedback from past supplier evaluations. Active autonomous-sourcing dialogue offsets the pause, but the pause is why this stays in the cautious band.

north_star_verdict.headline: Live build-vs-buy deal that Karen Pagorek paused on 11 May 2026 to deep-dive an unnamed external I/O provider; the relationship is genuinely two-way (Eduardo Viera email 12 Jun 2026, accepted 20 Jul connect) but the central proof point, the three-prong POC, is explicitly stalled and the 20 Jul reconnect is the gate that decides re-engage versus loss.
north_star_verdict.math: About 13 months in cycle, Shortlisted since 05 Dec 2025 (about 7 months in stage), close 22 Feb 2027 is 231 days out, so the date is not the binding constraint. The binding issue is momentum: the one moving workstream (the POC) was buyer-paused 11 May 2026, no commercial proposal is accepted, and no economic buyer has ever attended a call. One genuinely stalled action plus thinning cadence puts this in Slowing, not Off Track (a two-way email 12 Jun and an accepted 20 Jul connect keep it alive).
evidence: SF Next_Step (Karen Pagorek, 11 May 2026): the POC will not move forward, John Deere is re-assessing priorities, and is deep-diving another external I/O provider; a 20 Jul connect is accepted and the outcome could be re-engage.

Buyer incoming email 12 Jun 2026 (Eduardo Viera, via Clari): 'We are evaluating options and Zycus is one of those. Karen will be sharing more details in your next call.' confirms two-way engagement is live, not dark.
Best Case on a deal with a buyer-paused POC, no accepted commercial proposal, and zero economic-buyer meeting attendance is not defensible; the honest category is Pipeline.

metrics.narrative: The value thesis is hard-savings driven and buyer-articulated but still not modeled against John Deere's own baseline. Eduardo Viera confirmed on the 30 Sep 2025 1:1 that funding was 'secured' in 2024 and is backed by 'hard savings or hard numbers, incremental cost reduction and organizational optimization.' On the 18 Dec 2025 proposal review Karen Pagorek pressure-tested the headline ROI, calling the 40 percent sourcing-cycle reduction and the $48M savings claim 'pretty optimistic' and asking to 'reset based on what our current environment is.' Arthur Raguette offered a 70 to 80 data-point value-engineering benchmark (AP cycle time, sourcing time, contract time, compliance) versus Hackett and APQC, but that John-Deere-specific model has not been delivered, so quantified ROI against their baseline is the open move.

economic_buyer.narrative: Two budget authorities are mapped but neither is bought-in on a call. Eduardo Viera (Global Director, Supply Management Indirect Materials and Services, primary Executive Sponsor) is the practical economic buyer for this indirect-materials initiative: he controlled the partnership and make-versus-buy direction on 30 Sep 2025 ('for me, Zycus is that potential partner to accelerate what we don't have in our portfolio') and replied by email 12 Jun 2026, but his sponsorship is conditional and he has not pushed the deal internally. Joshua Jepsen (Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, Executive Sponsor on contact roles) holds ultimate budget authority for the $450k but has never appeared on any call or activity. The move is to convert Eduardo from a conditional sponsor into the broker who puts the value case in front of Jepsen.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Eduardo Viera PRIMARY Executive Sponsor; Joshua Jepsen Executive Sponsor)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit from the buyer. Karen Powers (24 Jun 2025) framed the decision as a make-versus-buy mix: keep partnerships for competitive-advantage use cases and purchase for 'things everybody does', with a hard requirement that any purchased tool fit John Deere's heavily customized ('deorized') processes and not force a step backward on efficiency. Karen Pagorek (18 Dec 2025) added commercial and proof criteria: transparent, defensible ROI against John Deere's actual environment (not headline benchmarks), a low-commitment way to test each module, and native integration (she asked to pull the bill of materials via API from their PLM rather than Excel, and iSaaS/SAP connectivity). Confirmed criteria: process fit, agentic differentiation over their SAP-UI build, credible savings math, and low-friction pilots.

decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence, John Deere is running an internally-led make-versus-buy evaluation, not a formal competitive tender: Eduardo Viera said on 30 Sep 2025 'at this point I don't think that we are looking at a competitive' and pointed to an on-site workshop as phase 3 of their own development. The process routes through the supply-chain digital-transformation org (Karen Powers, roughly 2,000 users) for tool selection and through Karen Pagorek for the strategic-sourcing pilot and commercials, with Eduardo as the sponsor who convenes the workshop and would bring the SAP technical team in. The process visibly slowed on 11 May 2026 when Pagorek paused the POC to re-assess priorities and deep-dive another provider; the 20 Jul connect is the next process checkpoint. The approval chain above Eduardo (to the relevant stakeholder) is named on contact roles but not yet walked.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are genuinely unknown because the deal has not reached commercials. The 18 Dec 2025 proposal was explicitly a 'budgetary estimate, not anywhere near a final commercial proposal' (Arthur Raguette), pricing on autonomous sourcing is still model-in-flux (spend-based tiers), and no MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal, procurement channel or InfoSec track has opened. A latent gating item is the SAP S/4HANA integration concern raised 18 Jun 2025 and the iSaaS integration scoping discussed 18 Dec 2025, which the SAP IT team will scrutinize. Visibility would come from the 20 Jul reconnect confirming whether John Deere will re-scope a paid pilot and name a procurement/legal owner.
sources: SF activity (SAP S/4HANA integration concern, 18 Jun 2025)

identify_pain.narrative: The operational and financial pain is well articulated by the buyer. Karen Powers (24 Jun 2025) described a primarily homegrown, heavily customized ('deorized') procurement stack (homegrown quoting tool 'TCS', Excel-based bidding) that John Deere now wants to shift toward purchased solutions to avoid falling behind on AI transformation: 'if you really want to go fast and transform from an AI standpoint, is building everything yourself the right direction? Probably not.' The pain owners are the roughly 2,000-user supply-chain organization and the indirect/MRO categories where ad-hoc, un-negotiated spend leaks 3 to 5 percent savings. Eduardo Viera (30 Sep 2025) reinforced the financial pain: the investment is justified on incremental hard-savings cost reduction and elimination of manual work. Confirmed pain; the open piece is quantifying it against their own baseline.

champion.narrative: Engagement is real but no power-champion is selling Zycus internally over the alternatives, which is why this reads developing/weak rather than strong. Karen Powers (Digital Product Manager, Global Supply Chain, Influencer) was the warm entry and praised the 24 Jun 2025 demo, but she enables tools rather than owning budget. Eduardo Viera (Executive Sponsor and primary contact) is the strongest advocate and the path to power, but his support is conditional on make-versus-buy fit and he has not driven the deal forward internally. Karen Pagorek (Global Strategic Sourcing Manager, Evaluator) became the day-to-day owner but is the person who paused the POC on 11 May 2026. The deal is effectively single-threaded through a rotating evaluator set; converting Eduardo into an active sponsor who brokers CFO Joshua Jepsen is what is missing.

competition.narrative: This is primarily a build-versus-buy contest with a fresh external entrant, not a classic suite bake-off. The strongest current threat is the unnamed external I/O provider John Deere began deep-diving on 11 May 2026, which directly triggered the POC pause; its identity is unknown and must be uncovered on 20 Jul. The persistent alternative is John Deere's in-house build (SAP-UI engine, iProcure, plus OpenAI/Ultrametrics/Worldlink partnerships) that Eduardo Viera said made 'tremendous progress' in 2025; the wedge is to position Merlin agentic intake and autonomous negotiation as modular accelerants over that build. GEP is the named peer threat in spend analysis (the three-prong plan pilots spend analysis 'to counter GEP'). Oracle and SAP Ariba are logged in Salesforce Competitors__c but absent from every recent call, so they rank low/dormant; the rep's Coupa reference on 18 Dec 2025 was a hypothetical, not a buyer preference.

deal_movement.summary: Momentum inverted since the 18 Dec 2025 proposal review. After a deep pricing/scoping session in December, Karen Pagorek paused the POC on 11 May 2026 and pushed the close from 30 Sep 2026 to 22 Feb 2027; the only live thread is an email exchange (Eduardo 12 Jun) and an accepted 20 Jul reconnect. Last genuine two-way buyer touch: Eduardo Viera's 12 Jun 2026 email.

items.change: Karen Pagorek paused the three-prong POC and said John Deere is re-assessing priorities while deep-diving another external I/O provider
Close date slipped from 30 Sep 2026 to 22 Feb 2027
Eduardo Viera replied by email confirming Zycus is one of the options being evaluated and Karen will share more on the next call
20 Jul reconnect with Karen Pagorek booked to diagnose the pause and next steps
Stage moved Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted and forecast Pipeline to Best Case

competitive_position.summary: Recency-weighted, the live threat is the unnamed external I/O provider John Deere began deep-diving on 11 May 2026 (it caused the POC pause), alongside John Deere's own in-house build that Eduardo confirmed made 'tremendous progress' in 2025. GEP is the dated peer threat in spend analysis. Oracle and SAP Ariba are logged in Salesforce but absent from all recent calls, so they rank low/dormant; Coupa was only a rep hypothetical and has faded.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers matter most now: uncover and neutralize the external I/O provider before 20 Jul, convert Eduardo Viera into an active sponsor who reaches CFO Jepsen, and reset ROI credibility with a John-Deere-specific value case.

champion_strength.summary: Engagement is real but no single power-champion is driving the deal for Zycus. Eduardo Viera is the strongest advocate (primary Executive Sponsor) but conditional on build-versus-buy fit and not pushing internally; Karen Powers is enthusiastic but lacks budget authority; Karen Pagorek owns the day-to-day but is the one who paused the POC on 11 May 2026.
champion_strength.champion: Eduardo Viera (developing); Karen Powers (enthusiastic, no budget power)
champion_strength.source: Avoma 1:1 30 Sep 2025; Avoma demo 24 Jun 2025; SF contact roles; SF Next_Step 11 May 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The Merlin agentic-intake and autonomous-negotiation story is landing in dialogue (Karen Pagorek pressed Arthur to disentangle e-auctions, tactical spend, and autonomous sourcing on 18 Dec 2025), so positioning is strong; the gap is proof, not narrative. The three-prong POC that would have converted the story to evidence is paused.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6.0 / Strong is corroborated by the calls: the buyer actively pressure-tested autonomous sourcing terminology and asked what a pilot would look like, and John Deere is explicitly running an AI-transformation mandate. The 11 May 2026 POC pause is a real down-signal but reflects prioritization, not lost AI appetite.

CFO Joshua Jepsen (ultimate budget) never engaged and Eduardo Viera's sponsorship is passive/conditional
Scope narrowed to a three-prong pilot and the POC paused amid a priority re-assessment, signalling a defensive buyer and a real do-nothing / in-house-build risk

Deep 'deorization' culture and homegrown-tool bias raise adoption and process-fit risk for any purchased suite

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has cooled from the December pricing depth to an email-only holding pattern with a paused POC; the deal is alive but the cadence has thinned and the next real signal is the 20 Jul connect.

scope_change.from: Full source-to-pay suite in the opportunity (12 modules incl. eProcurement, iContract, iRisk, invoice automation)
scope_change.to: Focused three-prong pilot: spend analysis, iSource strategic sourcing, and autonomous negotiation (ANA/MRO)
scope_change.detail: On 18 Dec 2025 Arthur and Karen Pagorek agreed to a phased approach that parked invoice automation ('I've left APIA very much out') and contract management ('not on the table'), and the 11 May 2026 three-prong plan (spend analysis to counter GEP, limited-scope iSource POC, ANA MRO pilot) narrowed the deal to a defensive, cost-conscious pilot rather than the full-suite buy.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: We are the shortlisted agentic front-runner in a build-versus-buy evaluation, but Karen Pagorek paused our POC on 11 May 2026 to deep-dive an unnamed external I/O provider, and the 20 Jul reconnect is the single gate between re-engagement and a slow loss to in-house build.

win_position.text: Win sits in the low-50s not higher because, despite strong agentic fit and an AIS of 6/Strong, the buyer paused our only proof point on 11 May 2026, no economic buyer has ever joined a call, and the scope has narrowed to a defensive three-prong pilot.

Zycus is genuinely shortlisted and differentiated on agentic intake and autonomous negotiation: Karen Pagorek engaged deeply on autonomous-sourcing pricing and terminology on 18 Dec 2025, and Eduardo Viera named Zycus 'that potential partner to accelerate what we don't have' on 30 Sep 2025.

The competitive risk is real and current: an unnamed external I/O provider John Deere began deep-diving on 11 May 2026 triggered the pause, while their in-house SAP-UI build (which made 'tremendous progress' in 2025) is the standing do-nothing alternative.

Power access is thin: CFO Joshua Jepsen owns the $450k budget but has never engaged, and champion Eduardo Viera is conditional and passive, so there is no one selling Zycus internally against the alternatives.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum inverted after the 18 Dec 2025 pricing session: the POC was buyer-paused on 11 May 2026 and the close slipped from 30 Sep 2026 to 22 Feb 2027.
The relationship is still two-way: Eduardo Viera emailed on 12 Jun 2026 that Zycus is one of the options being evaluated and an accepted 20 Jul reconnect is on the calendar.
Engagement is now email-only with no live workstream; the customer named a 'complete eval in 2 months' timeline on 11 May but the last actual working call was 18 Dec 2025.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is low and defensive: Karen Pagorek said 'the POC will not move forward' and that John Deere is 're-assessing priorities' on 11 May 2026.
The buyer keeps the door open, accepting the 20 Jul connect and asking Zycus to stay engaged rather than closing the evaluation.
No commercial proposal is accepted and no economic buyer has sponsored a spend, so there is no forward contractual commitment yet.

deal_risk.text: The primary threat is inertia and in-house build: 'nothing runs like a Deere' deorization culture plus a paused POC means the likeliest failure is a no-decision, not a competitor win.
An unnamed external I/O provider is actively being deep-dived and its identity is unknown, so we cannot yet counter-position it before 20 Jul.
ROI credibility is exposed: Karen Pagorek called the $48M savings claim 'pretty optimistic' on 18 Dec 2025 and the John-Deere-specific value-engineering case is still undelivered.

champion_strength.evidence: No power-champion selling internally; Eduardo Viera conditional/passive, Karen Pagorek paused the POC (11 May 2026)

customer_preference.evidence: POC paused and John Deere deep-diving another external I/O provider (11 May 2026)

deal_momentum.evidence: Buyer-paused POC and close slip 30 Sep 2026 to 22 Feb 2027; email-only holding pattern

customer_preference.evidence: Karen Pagorek (11 May 2026): 'the POC will not move forward', John Deere is re-assessing priorities and deep-diving another external I/O provider; engagement is now positive-but-passive rather than a Zycus lean.

business_case.evidence: Strong qualitative pain (deorized homegrown stack, ad-hoc MRO savings leak, AI-transformation mandate) and Eduardo's hard-savings funding thesis, but the headline $48M/40% ROI was challenged as 'optimistic' and no John-Deere-baseline model has been delivered.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 18 is anchored on the stage win (42), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 32. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Win sits in the low-50s not higher because, despite strong agentic fit and an AIS of 6/Strong, the buyer paused our only proof point on 11 May 2026, no economic buyer has ever joined a call, and the scope has narrowed to a defensive three-prong pilot.

Zycus is genuinely shortlisted and differentiated on agentic intake and autonomous negotiation: Karen Pagorek engaged deeply on autonomous-sourcing pricing and terminology on 18 Dec 2025, and Eduardo Viera named Zycus 'that potential partner to accelerate what we don't have' on 30 Sep 2025.

The competitive risk is real and current: an unnamed external I/O provider John Deere began deep-diving on 11 May 2026 triggered the pause, while their in-house SAP-UI build (which made 'tremendous progress' in 2025) is the standing do-nothing alternative.

Power access is thin: CFO Joshua Jepsen owns the $450k budget but has never engaged, and champion Eduardo Viera is conditional and passive, so there is no one selling Zycus internally against the alternatives.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Thin signals pull the read to 42.

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: Momentum inverted after the 18 Dec 2025 pricing session: the POC was buyer-paused on 11 May 2026 and the close slipped from 30 Sep 2026 to 22 Feb 2027.
The relationship is still two-way: Eduardo Viera emailed on 12 Jun 2026 that Zycus is one of the options being evaluated and an accepted 20 Jul reconnect is on the calendar.
Engagement is now email-only with no live workstream; the customer named a 'complete eval in 2 months' timeline on 11 May but the last actual working call was 18 Dec 2025.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

bullets.text: The primary threat is inertia and in-house build: 'nothing runs like a Deere' deorization culture plus a paused POC means the likeliest failure is a no-decision, not a competitor win.
An unnamed external I/O provider is actively being deep-dived and its identity is unknown, so we cannot yet counter-position it before 20 Jul.
ROI credibility is exposed: Karen Pagorek called the $48M savings claim 'pretty optimistic' on 18 Dec 2025 and the John-Deere-specific value-engineering case is still undelivered.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Recency-weighted, the live threat is the unnamed external I/O provider John Deere began deep-diving on 11 May 2026 (it caused the POC pause), alongside John Deere's own in-house build that Eduardo confirmed made 'tremendous progress' in…

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: Commitment is low and defensive: Karen Pagorek said 'the POC will not move forward' and that John Deere is 're-assessing priorities' on 11 May 2026.
The buyer keeps the door open, accepting the 20 Jul connect and asking Zycus to stay engaged rather than closing the evaluation.
No commercial proposal is accepted and no economic buyer has sponsored a spend, so there is no forward contractual commitment yet.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Hold the 20 Jul reconnect with Karen Pagorek to diagnose the POC pause and name the external provider. (by 20 Jul)
Deliver the overdue John-Deere-specific value-engineering case to reset the $48M ROI credibility. (by 17 Jul)
Broker a direct meeting with Eduardo Viera to re-secure sponsorship and re-scope the three-prong POC. (by 31 Jul)

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Activities 120d; field history to 05 Dec 2025; contact roles full
gaps: Identity of the external I/O provider John Deere is deep-diving (named only as 'another external I/O provider', 11 May 2026)
CFO Joshua Jepsen has never attended a call, so his stance on the $450k is unknown
No John-Deere-baseline ROI model has been delivered, so quantified savings remain unvalidated
